page: Microsoft Azure F64alds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Faldsv7 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. This x86_64 architecture server features 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs running at a high clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The system is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It includes 7559 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not include any GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the F64alds_v7 demonstrates top-tier performance, placing in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests. Its memory subsystem achieves top-tier read and write bandwidth, with uncached large-block reads reaching 415.58 GB/sec. Computational efficiency is further highlighted by top-tier results in Passmark CPU tests, static web serving throughput at 96.82 GB/sec, and high-speed Redis SET operations. The processor's support for AVX-512 and vector neural network instructions (VNNI) enables strong performance in lightweight LLM inference, processing 7B model prompts at 298.62 tokens/sec.

The primary architectural tradeoff of the F64alds_v7 is its low memory density of 2.0 GB per vCPU, which makes it less suitable for memory-intensive enterprise applications or massive in-memory databases. However, this resource allocation delivers high qualitative cost efficiency for compute-bound workloads, as users do not pay for idle memory overhead. Combined with its substantial 7559 GB local storage, this server is highly optimized for high-throughput web serving, batch processing, CPU-based machine learning inference, and database operations that require high IOPS and raw computational power.
description: Microsoft Azure F64alds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Faldsv7 family, featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it is equipped with 128.0 GB of memory, providing a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The server includes 7559 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, but lacks GPU acceleration. In benchmark testing, the F64alds_v7 delivers top-tier CPU and memory bandwidth performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng and Passmark evaluations. It also excels in static web serving and small-to-medium LLM inference. The low memory-to-core ratio presents a tradeoff for memory-heavy applications, but offers high qualitative cost efficiency for compute-bound tasks. This server is best suited for high-throughput web serving, batch processing, and storage-heavy database workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F64alds_v7 is a compute-optimized server with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 7559 GB local storage, delivering top-tier CPU and database benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F64alds_v7 features 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 7559 GB local storage, optimized for high-compute and database workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency 64-core AMD processor virtual machine with dense local storage, optimized for compute-heavy and high-throughput database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 64-core AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz.
Equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, featuring a compute-optimized 2.0 GB per core ratio.
Includes 7559 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Delivers top-tier CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance.
Supports AVX-512 and VNNI instruction sets for accelerated vector operations.
Best suited for high-throughput web serving, batch processing, and database workloads.
